title: Dao of the Celestial Blade
author: Azure Ink
authorBio: Azure Ink is a Xianxia author weaving tales of cultivation, destiny, and divine ambition.
coverAlt: Dao of the Celestial Blade novel cover — a young cultivator holding a glowing jade pendant under moonlight
description: Lin Feng was the weakest disciple in the Seven Stars Sect — until a mysterious jade pendant awakened the memories of a fallen Sword God. Now he must walk the razor's edge between mortal loyalty and divine ambition, while ancient enemies stir in the shadows of the cultivation world. A tale of hidden power, ancient legacies, and the price of awakening a god's memories inside a mortal's body.

title: The Last Oracle of Shanghai
author: Midnight Quill
authorBio: Midnight Quill writes urban supernatural fiction set in the neon-lit streets of modern Asia, where ancient forces collide with contemporary life.
coverAlt: The Last Oracle of Shanghai novel cover — a fortune teller in a neon-lit Shanghai antique shop
description: Chen Wei runs a tiny antique shop by day. By night, she reads fortunes that always come true. When a billionaire CEO walks in demanding to know his death date, she uncovers a conspiracy spanning three generations — and her own buried past beneath the glittering skyline of Shanghai. A supernatural mystery where fate, fortune, and family secrets collide in modern China.

title: System Crash: Reboot Protocol
author: Zero Day
authorBio: Zero Day crafts immersive LitRPG sci-fi where virtual worlds blur with reality, exploring what happens when the game becomes more real than the world outside.
coverAlt: System Crash Reboot Protocol novel cover — a VR headset glowing with corrupted code in a dark room
description: When the world's first full-dive VRMMO goes live, 10 million players log in. But the logout button is gone. Kai, a veteran beta tester, discovers the game's source code is rewriting itself — and the NPCs are becoming self-aware. What began as a game has become a fight for survival. A LitRPG thriller where the line between game and reality shatters, and the players must adapt or be deleted.
